origin.name: Ghana
origin.death_registration: Births and Deaths Registry of Ghana
origin.key_document: Ghanaian death certificate (Births and Deaths Registry, in English)
origin.source: FCDO Ghana travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Accra guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Ghanaian death certificate (Births and Deaths Registry)
special_rules: Documentation is in English throughout. No translation required.
Deaths must be registered with the Births and Deaths Registry before any other step.
Police report required for unexpected or unattended deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Ghanaian documentation is in English and typically accepted directly. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Accra can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Ghana to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Ghana. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Births and Deaths Registry, police report, and Accra cargo flights. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
special_rules: Irish Embassy in Accra handles consular matters.
Documentation in English. No translation required.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Documentation in English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Accra can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Ghana to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Ghana and needs to come home to Ireland. 2-4 weeks. Accra cargo and all documentation handled. Contact us 24/7.
